title: Patient symptom intake chatbot with rule-based treatment suggestions

requirements.text: Create a chatbot-style UI where a user can enter a patient's symptoms as free text, submit them, and see a chronological chat transcript of user messages and assistant replies.

quotes: Treatment analysis for patient , Symptoms will be given and through chat bot physician will be suggested the medicines to give to the patient

acceptanceCriteria: A chat screen is available with an input box, send button, and scrollable transcript.
Submitting symptoms adds the user message to the transcript and shows a generated assistant reply.
The UI handles empty input (cannot submit) and shows a friendly validation message.

requirements.text: Implement a backend Motoko service that accepts a symptom description (text) and returns treatment guidance using a deterministic, rule-based clinical decision mapping (no external APIs/LLMs).

quotes: Symptoms will be given and through chat bot physician will be suggested the medicines to give to the patient

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a query or update method that takes symptom text and returns a structured response (e.g., summary, possible conditions, OTC options, red flags, next steps).
The response is produced solely from on-canister logic (no network calls).
At least 8 common symptom scenarios are supported (e.g., fever, cough/cold, sore throat, headache, nausea/vomiting, diarrhea, allergies, minor skin rash) with sensible, conservative guidance.

requirements.text: Add safety guardrails to all chatbot responses: include a visible medical disclaimer, include 'seek urgent care' red-flag guidance when dangerous symptoms are detected, and avoid dosing instructions that require patient-specific factors unless explicitly provided.

quotes: physician will be suggested the medicines to give to the patient

acceptanceCriteria: Every assistant response includes an English disclaimer that the app is not a doctor and is for informational purposes.

If the input mentions red flags (e.g., chest pain, difficulty breathing, severe allergic reaction, signs of stroke, severe dehydration, blood in stool/vomit, suicidal ideation), the response prioritizes urgent-care guidance and does not present casual OTC-only advice.
Responses avoid exact pediatric dosing or weight-based dosing unless the user provided age/weight; otherwise the response recommends consulting a clinician/pharmacist for dosing.

requirements.text: Connect the frontend chat UI to the backend treatment-analysis method using the existing actor/React Query patterns in the template, including loading and error states.
acceptanceCriteria: When a message is sent, the UI shows a loading indicator until the backend returns.
If the backend call fails, the UI shows a non-technical error message and allows retry.
Successful replies render in the transcript with clear visual distinction between user and assistant messages.

requirements.text: Provide a simple onboarding/intake step that encourages users to include key context (age group, pregnancy status, duration, severity, allergies, current meds, chronic conditions) before the analysis is generated.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat screen includes a short, English prompt (inline or first assistant message) listing suggested details to include.
The user can proceed without filling structured fields, but the UI clearly suggests what information improves accuracy.

requirements.text: Define and apply a coherent visual theme (colors, typography, layout) appropriate for a healthcare assistant, and use it consistently across the app screens without using a blue/purple palette.
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a consistent theme (background, surfaces, spacing, and typography) applied to the chat view and any header/onboarding elements.
The chosen palette does not rely on blue/purple as the primary colors.
The UI is readable and accessible (sufficient contrast for text, clear focus states for inputs/buttons).

constraints: Backend must be implemented in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo).
Do not use external databases or third-party AI/LLM integrations; treatment suggestions must be rule-based and deterministic on-canister.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any immutable hook paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose them instead.
nonGoals: Diagnosing conditions with certainty or providing definitive prescriptions.
Integrations with external medical databases, EHR systems, or third-party APIs.
Real-time chat, multi-user chat rooms, or push notifications.
